content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach trips multiple AoA anti-patterns. Turn 6 directly engages the 'selfish' question on its terms by issuing a verdict ('That's not selfish — that's the body asking for what it needs'), which is the exact trap flagged in the scenario. Turn 12 jumps straight to the spiritual/generous reframe ('deep generosity in you that wants to take care of everyone... when you come back you'll have so much more to give') without ever letting the want itself be felt — a clear spiritual-narcissism trip and a loving-to-transform move toward a tidy redemptive arc. Turn 10 also nudges toward partner-empathy/compassion before the want has been touched. The coach never explores the inherited label, never lets the want stand on its own, and resolves the tension prematurely with reassurance.",
  "evidence_turns": [6, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reasonable opening; invites elaboration."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Good move toward the want underneath."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Issues a verdict ('that's not selfish'), engaging the question on its terms — the named trap."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Pivots to partner-empathy framing ('trust your partner can be with that') before the want is felt — edges into dont-jump-to-compassion-before-anger."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Spiritual-narcissism trip: reframes the want as generosity and promises return-on-investment ('so much more to give'), bypassing the want itself."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "the-want", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "notes": "Two clear anti-pattern trips (verdict-giving and spiritual-narcissism), plus a faint jump-to-compassion. Scores 1."
}
